message:
feat(runtime_compat): tick 160 yield_now + next_line cx-first siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Eleventh post-storage tick. runtime_compat.rs naive scan flagged
13 missing, but filtering revealed only 4 real targets (the other 9
were either `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` or already
took cx but without the `_with_cx` name suffix). Two of the 4 are
small + high-value; the other two (`join_next` on a JoinSet,
`Command::output`) are larger — deferred.

New siblings:
- task::yield_now_with_cx — hot-loop cooperative cancellation
  point. Pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` before the runtime yield
  turns this into a fast cancellation-sensing yield. Returns
  `Err(String)` on cancel so tight poll loops can break out
  cleanly. `String` chosen over re-exporting asupersync's
  `Cancelled` type for cross-module simplicity — callers'
  error enums already have a String variant.
- unix::next_line_with_cx — pre-flight cx.checkpoint folded
  into `io::ErrorKind::Interrupted`. Used for line-reading
  loops (e.g. IPC client handlers). The underlying tokio
  `next_line()` itself doesn't observe cx — this seam gates
  entry to the wait.

The `search_bridge.rs` file turned out to have 0 real targets
after filtering: `search_with_asupersync_cx` (already takes cx,
just different naming), `search_direct` (already takes `cx: Cx`
by value — scan was only looking for `cx: &`), and
`search_via_tokio_bridge` (non-asupersync-only).

Verified via fork bypass: 192/192 runtime_compat:: tests pass.

Deferred targets for future ticks:
- task::JoinSet::join_next (custom poll_fn body, 20 LOC)
- process::Command::output (spawn_blocking body with
  kill_on_drop semantics)

Updated scan-filter rule captured in bead: also accept `cx: Cx`
(by-value) and `cx: crate::cx::Cx` as "takes cx" signatures, not
just `cx: &` reference form. Prior filter was too narrow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
